name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Proxy Execution Via Explorer
notes:
This Building Block the use of explorer.exe for evading defense mechanisms.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative
SIGMA Title: Proxy Execution Via Explorer.exe
SIGMA Description: Attackers can use explorer.exe for evading defense mechanisms


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Proxy Execution Via Explorer on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of explorer.exe
when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of cmd.exe
when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%explorer.exe%' AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Proxy Execution Via Explorer on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of explorer.exe
AND when the event matches Parent Process Name (custom) is any of cmd.exe
AND when the event matches LOWER("Command") LIKE '%explorer.exe%' AQL filter query
